claim: AI-generated unit tests often become self-affirming - they verify what code does rather than what users experience, missing real bugs
actionable: Use Playwright for functional/browser tests FIRST (red), then generate code to pass them (green), avoiding self-affirming unit tests
source_title: Beyond Code Coverage: Functionality Testing with Playwright MCP — Marlene Mhangami, Microsoft

claim: Clean codebases amplify AI gains while unchecked AI amplifies entropy - a case study showed unchecked AI increased PR volume but decreased code quality, yielding only 1% productivity gain
actionable: Enforce clean codebase practices (test coverage, type coverage, modularity) before deploying agentic coding at scale
source_title: Beyond Code Coverage: Functionality Testing with Playwright MCP — Marlene Mhangami, Microsoft

claim: Red-Green-Refactor TDD with AI: generate failing Playwright tests (red), fast code to pass (green), spend most time refactoring for quality
actionable: Structure AI-assisted TDD to: 1) Write functional tests first, 2) Let agent generate code quickly, 3) Refactor for quality
source_title: Beyond Code Coverage: Functionality Testing with Playwright MCP — Marlene Mhangami, Microsoft

claim: Playwright MCP server enables agents to write and run browser tests that validate actual user interactions in real-time
actionable: Install Playwright MCP server in Claude/Copilot to enable agents to verify features via browser automation
source_title: Beyond Code Coverage: Functionality Testing with Playwright MCP — Marlene Mhangami, Microsoft
